qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IDOR in Shop API address update endpoint allows cross-customer address modification and ownership takeover
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the authenticated Shop API address update endpoint and a missing per-address ownership check in packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Http/Controllers/API/AddressController.php; the advisory targets unauthenticated installer API routes in packages/Ibkul/Installer/src/Routes/Ib.php that remain callable after installation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Bagisto installer API missing-authentication root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an authenticated customer-address IDOR in a different package, endpoint, controller, and authorization check.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6h7w-v2xr-mqvw describes unauthenticated direct access to /install/api/* endpoints declared in packages/Ibkul/Installer/src/Routes/Ib.php, including /install/api/admin-config-setup creating admin accounts without authentication.
Final session manifest lists idor-address-update as the only qualifying_vuln final finding.

idor-address-update reports PUT /api/customer/addresses/edit in packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Http/Controllers/API/AddressController.php, requiring an authenticated customer and missing ownership verification for request('id').
